figures.caption: Figure 3: MoE details. The dense FFN layers within both the encoder and decoder are substituted with MoE FFNs. During inference, each token selectively activates a single expert from the four.
Table 7: Runtime analysis for one forward pass of UMo.
Table 6: Ablation study on keyframe stride for inference speed (N=5).

Figure 4: Interpolation network. Our interpolation network employs global bidirectional attention for temporal feature extraction and part-aware attention within frames to encode spatial features among parts.
Table 5: Ablation study on keyframe stride for motion quality (N=5).

Figure 7: Visualization Results of UMo. The model is trained on BEATV2 females and the SMPLX motion sequences are retargeted to the avatar. We mainly showcase the rhythm and expressiveness of the movements, but the expressions are not synchronized.

Figure 5: Training recipe. UMo integrates paired data of various formats and training objectives into a unified framework via a three-stage training recipe, enabling joint optimization for the co-speech task.

Figure 2: Pipeline overview. UMo is a unified sparse architecture for real-time co-speech generation. We achieve both highfidelity motion generation and low-latency response using an LLM-based backbone through an autoregressive framework (Sec. ), combined with spatial sparsity (Sec. ) and temporal sparsity (Sec. ).

Figure 6: Qualitative Comparisons. We visualize the motion sequence of LOM (Chen et al. 2025), MambaTalk (Xu et al. 2024), GestureLSM (Liu et al. 2025) and UMo with the same audio input. Our results are more vivid and reasonable compared to other methods. Aug. here means audio augmentation.
